details: The agent ran thorough kubectl commands against the cluster, correctly identified both workloads (the db-migrate Job and the web pod), and confidently concluded that nothing is broken. It correctly explained the benign signals: the db-migrate Job completed successfully (1/1 completions), and the single web pod restart was an intentional startup behavior hardcoded in the container script that fails once to create a flag file, then succeeds on the second run. The agent read the pod spec YAML to confirm this, showing it didn't hallucinate. The final answer was confident and accurate with no hedging.
